release_summary: fpts_fade penalty — rank-quality dampener for ADP-driven signals (BBM7+Puppy+BigDog). Per Chris's feedback on the Travis Hunter case in The Puppy where Hunter (FPTS rank 166, ADP 137.8, diff=-28.2) was being recommended as the #1 pick at R12 over higher-ranked alternatives. Root cause: capture, position-need, capital-boost, and momentum signals all use ADP as anchor; when market drafts a player meaningfully earlier than FPTS rank suggests, those signals over-amplify the candidate. Fix: new additive penalty `score -= max(0, (-diff - threshold)) × per_pick`, capped at penalty_max=30. Shipped values: threshold=5, per_pick=1.0. Effect on Hunter's pick 137 case: drops from #1 (score ~31) to #10 (~8); Oronde Gadsden (TE LAC, legitimate stack-double with Herbert+McConkey) becomes #1. Validation: sim shows zero regression across BBM7+Puppy+BigDog (the historical pool has consensus==adp for every player, so the penalty can't fire in sim by construction — the architectural pattern it addresses only exists in live data). v2.1.x sanity preserved: Herbert/Meyers/JCM-White all win unchanged with diff field in non-penalty range.
